findings.title: Quote replacement reintroduces sanitized characters
findings.evidence: The post-image order normalizes existing backslashes first and then expands quotes with a replacement containing new backslashes and a double quote. For an input containing a quote, `clean` can still include the same character classes the new sanitization step appears to handle.
findings.suggested_comment: `Process` now replaces backslashes before running `clean = strings.Replace(clean, "\"", "\\'\\\"\\\\", -1)`, but that second replacement introduces fresh backslashes and a double quote after the backslash-normalization pass. If `clean` is expected to remove or normalize those characters before printing, please use a replacement that preserves that invariant or run a context-aware escaping/encoding step instead.
findings.fix_hint: Clarify the intended output invariant for `clean`; if quotes/backslashes must not survive, avoid a replacement literal that contains them or apply the final escaping with an encoder for the target context.
